description_long: This sculpture depicts a group of six life-sized human figures standing in a tight circle, their bodies closely intertwined and facing inward. The figures are nude, with detailed anatomical features and naturalistic postures, suggesting a sense of intimacy and unity. Each figure stands on a small, individual pedestal that is part of a shared, roughly circular base. The surface of the sculpture appears weathered and textured, with a patina that gives it a mottled, stone-like appearance. The overall composition emphasizes the connection between the figures, with their arms and shoulders gently touching or overlapping. The base is irregular and appears to be integrated with the ground, anchoring the group firmly in place.
description: A life-sized sculpture of six nude human figures standing in a close circle, their bodies intertwined and sharing a textured, circular base.
description_view_0: This view shows the backs and sides of several figures, highlighting their muscular detail and the close proximity of their bodies.
description_view_1: This angle reveals more of the faces and chests of the figures, emphasizing their inward-facing arrangement and the sense of group unity.
description_view_2: From this perspective, the backs and shoulders of the figures are most prominent, with heads slightly tilted toward the center.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the rear and side profiles of the figures, showing the curvature of their spines and the overlapping arms.
attribute_location_context: No specific environmental context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated on a neutral background.
